title: JSON-LD, Structured Data, Open Graph, Social Metadata, and Non-Guarantees · Runtime Lens: Social Metadata Contract
summary: Malformed JSON-LD is silently accepted, remote contexts are fetched, structured claims conflict with visible content, and complete metadata is reported as a guaranteed rich result. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the social metadata contract increment in structured and social metadata evidence report. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Audit Open Graph and other selected social fields by declared platform contract, URL identity, image evidence, locale, and fallback behavior.
Compare structured claims with visible page evidence and flag missing, contradictory, hidden, stale, or unverifiable values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example site-quality team handles case 6410 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate bounded JSON-LD, schema and visible-content evidence, social metadata, and rich-result non-guarantees; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Audit Open Graph and other selected social fields by declared platform contract, URL identity, image evidence, locale, and fallback behavior. A defensible solution applies Social Metadata Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to structured and social metadata evidence report.

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-social-metadata-contract — Audit Open Graph and other selected social fields by declared platform contract, URL identity, image evidence, locale, and fallback behavior.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-structured-social-metadata-2
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-structured-social-metadata-2.
# Case 7417: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def worked_scraper_structured_social_metadata_2(documents: int = 20, parsed_documents: int = 17, rejected_documents: int = 3, visible_conflicts: int = 4, remote_context_fetches: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorizationworked2",
        "url7417",
        "response2dekrow",
        "treeworked27417",
        "frontier7417worked2",
        "report2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or parsed_documents < 0 or rejected_documents < 0:
        return False, documents, "metadata-count"
    if parsed_documents + rejected_documents != documents:
        return False, parsed_documents, "metadata-reconciliation"
    if visible_conflicts < 0:
        return False, visible_conflicts, "visible-content-evidence"
    if remote_context_fetches > 0:
        return False, remote_context_fetches, "remote-context-fetch"
    assert parsed_documents + rejected_documents == documents and remote_context_fetches == 0
    return True, parsed_documents, "bounded-structured-metadata"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python crawler decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected authorization, URL, response, tree, record, frontier, browser, report, or recovery case, then state which network, owner, robots, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, accessibility, privacy, security, legal, load, schedule, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-social-metadata-contract-11t0lu
# Competency: scraper-social-metadata-contract.
# Case 6872: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def evidence_scraper_social_metadata_contract_11t0lu(documents: int = 20, parsed_documents: int = 17, rejected_documents: int = 3, visible_conflicts: int = 4, remote_context_fetches: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_11t0lu = "11t0lu-ul0t11-6872"
    assert evidence_variant_11t0lu
    evidence_axes_11t0lu = (
        "authorization11t0lu",
        "url6872",
        "responseul0t11",
        "tree11t0lu6872",
        "frontier687211t0lu",
        "reportul0t116872",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_11t0lu)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or parsed_documents < 0 or rejected_documents < 0:
        return False, documents, "metadata-count"
    if parsed_documents + rejected_documents != documents:
        return False, parsed_documents, "metadata-reconciliation"
    if visible_conflicts < 0:
        return False, visible_conflicts, "visible-content-evidence"
    if remote_context_fetches > 0:
        return False, remote_context_fetches, "remote-context-fetch"
    assert parsed_documents + rejected_documents == documents and remote_context_fetches == 0
    return True, parsed_documents, "bounded-structured-metadata"

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Sequence Social Metadata Contract evidence · Theory Social Metadata…, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Social Metadata Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Sequence Social Metadata Contract evidence · Theory Social Metadata…, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 6135 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate bounded JSON-LD, schema and visible-content evidence, social metadata, and rich-result non-guarantees; the target owner must be able to pause the crawl; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Audit Open Graph and other selected social fields by declared platform contract, URL identity, image evidence, locale, and fallback behavior.

For Authorized crawling and Python Sequence Social Metadata Contract evidence · Theory Social Metadata…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: One universal set of social tags is interpreted identically by every platform.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Social Metadata Contract model · Theory Social Metadata C…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Social Metadata Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 4516 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate bounded JSON-LD, schema and visible-content evidence, social metadata, and rich-result non-guarantees; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Audit Open Graph and other selected social fields by declared platform contract, URL identity, image evidence, locale, and fallback behavior.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Social Metadata Contract model · Theory Social Metadata C….
steps.why: Retrieving Social Metadata Contract through the concrete structured and social metadata evidence report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Social Metadata Contract model · Theory Social Metadata C…, keep Social Metadata Contract connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.
